title: Crumbly Canadian Seaweed Crumble
description: A fermented and crumbly seaweed dish with a mixture powder that is pescatarian-friendly, perfect for adventurous palates.

ingredients.name: Seaweed Powder
ingredients.notes: Use a non-fermented variety as per pescatarian diet

ingredients.name: Coconut Oil
ingredients.notes: Opt for MCT oil or other low-medium heat safe oils

ingredients.name: Almond Flour
ingredients.notes: Use gluten-free almond flour if necessary

ingredients.name: Coconut Sugar
ingredients.notes: Adjust sweetness based on taste preference

ingredients.name: Fermented Miso Paste
ingredients.notes: Optional for fermented taste, pescatarian option

instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine seaweed powder, coconut oil, almond flour, coconut sugar, vanilla bean powder, and fermented miso paste (if using). Stir well to ensure all ingredients are evenly distributed.
Slowly add water or vegan milk while continuously stirring until you achieve a crumbly consistency that can be spread thinly. This may require some kneading for better texture.
Transfer the mixture onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Use a spoon or spatula to create layers, making sure each layer is evenly distributed and thin.
Bake at 180°C (350°F) for about 20-25 minutes or until golden brown on top. Keep an eye as it can easily burn. Let cool completely before serving.
